summary: Recent updates to the Web Speech technology have focused on improving the way speech recognition and audio interactions are managed within the Firefox browser. By cleaning up the task lifecycle, the browser can more efficiently handle speech-related operations, leading to a smoother user experience. Additionally, the ability to suspend and resume both Web Audio and Web Speech when audio focus changes ensures seamless transitions, such as when users switch between different media sources. Finally, moving media control handling to the content process enhances performance and reliability. **These changes collectively aim to provide a more responsive and integrated speech interaction experience for users.**
